creator: joel@tahoestores.com
text:
pkg version -v reports:

gpu-firmware-intel-kmod-broxton-20230210_1 <   needs updating (index has 20230625)
gpu-firmware-intel-kmod-elkhartlake-20230210_1 <   needs updating (index has 20230625)
gpu-firmware-intel-kmod-geminilake-20230210_1 <   needs updating (index has 20230625)
gpu-firmware-intel-kmod-icelake-20230210_1 <   needs updating (index has 20230625)
gpu-firmware-intel-kmod-kabylake-20230210_1 <   needs updating (index has 20230625)
gpu-firmware-intel-kmod-rocketlake-20230210_1 <   needs updating (index has 20230625)
gpu-firmware-intel-kmod-tigerlake-20230210_1 <   needs updating (index has 20230625)

These ports all point back to gpu-firmware-intel-kmod.

So I rebuild gpu-firmware-intel-kmod -> /usr/ports/graphics/gpu-firmware-intel-kmod 

and pkg version -v still reports:

gpu-firmware-intel-kmod-broxton-20230210_1 <   needs updating (index has 20230625)
gpu-firmware-intel-kmod-elkhartlake-20230210_1 <   needs updating (index has 20230625)
gpu-firmware-intel-kmod-geminilake-20230210_1 <   needs updating (index has 20230625)
gpu-firmware-intel-kmod-icelake-20230210_1 <   needs updating (index has 20230625)
gpu-firmware-intel-kmod-kabylake-20230210_1 <   needs updating (index has 20230625)
gpu-firmware-intel-kmod-rocketlake-20230210_1 <   needs updating (index has 20230625)
gpu-firmware-intel-kmod-tigerlake-20230210_1 <   needs updating (index has 20230625)

This happens on all gpu versions.

Please advise.
